text:
Once upon a time in the ancient forest of Whispering Oaks...

A young squirrel named Olive loved to climb the tallest oaks. One bright afternoon, fueled by mischief, she hopped up after a shiny pink bead.

Down in a cozy hollow lived an old owl. Olive sang to him with her tiny cheeks full.

"Stop singing!" chuckled the owl. “You’ll scare away your own feathers that night."

Just then a glow grew big outside. Olive fluttered out...a soft glow.

"It's moonlight! I got it!" she proclaimed.

But the owl looked skeptical.

She flew in circles with it.

“Moon magic,” winked the owl.

By dawn, the forest was peaceful.

Olive flew cheerily back to the oak.

That morning was special. Everything seemed peaceful.

Then strange wings swooped down. An eagle? Something darker.

"Who goes there?"

"I mean none!"

Just an owl. Olive swooped.

"No feathers."

She took off.

They locked
